data.title: HPE stock drops after fiscal 2026 guidance misses expectations, attributed to slowing growth in networking business.
data.description: HPE's stock experienced a decline following the release of its fiscal 2026 guidance, which was lower than anticipated. This drop was primarily due to a deceleration in growth within its networking segment. The disappointing outlook led to a sell-off of HPE shares.

data.title: ClassPass partners with The Manner hotel in SoHo, NYC giving guests credits for fitness experiences.
data.description: Starting from today, guests staying at the downtown hotel in New York City will be offered ClassPass credits with each visit. These credits will grant access to a variety of fitness, wellness, and lifestyle experiences throughout the city. The initiative aims to enhance the guests' experience by providing them with opportunities to explore the city's top offerings. ClassPass is a popular lifestyle app that offers access to a wide range of fitness and wellness activities, and this collaboration is set to benefit those staying at the downtown hotel. The partnership is part of the hotel's efforts to cater to guest preferences and create a unique experience.
Fed's Miran: The longer policy stays restrictive, greater the downside.

data.title: Experienced mining executive Paul Richardson joins board as a non-executive director.
data.description: Paul Richardson has been appointed as a Non-Executive Director.

data.title: BNY Mellon (NYSE: BK) saw record Q3 revenue at $5.081 billion, with $1.91 adjusted EPS, surpassing estimates. Notable rises in fee revenue and net interest income, pre-tax operating margin at 36%, and steady growth in deposits and loans. Tangible book value per share at $30.60. Read more on Benzinga.com.
data.description: BNY (NYSE: BK) posted third-quarter 2025 diluted EPS at $1.88 and adjusted diluted EPS at $1.91, surpassing the estimated $1.77. The total revenue increased by 9% to $5.081 billion compared to the estimated $4.974 billion, fueled by a 7% rise in fee revenue and an 18% surge in net interest income. GAAP pre-tax operating margin stood at 36%, return on equity at 13.7%, and return on tangible common equity at 25.6%. Average deposits amounted to $299.3 billion, up by 5% from the previous year, while average loans grew by 5%, reaching $72.69 billion. The net interest margin increased to 1.31%, a 15 basis points rise from the previous year. The provision for credit losses experienced a $7 million benefit. Furthermore, the book value per share was $55.99, and tangible book value per share was $30.60. The bank's CET1 capital totaled $20.6 billion.

data.title: Dallas-Plano-Irving home prices drop by 1.4% year-over-year in September, as per First American Data & Analytics report.
data.description: First American Data & Analytics, a division of First American Financial Corporation, has published its September 2025 Home Price Index (HPI) report. This report, which provides property-centric information, risk management, and valuation solutions, tracks home price changes nationally, statewide, and at the metropolitan level. The data, which is updated less than four weeks behind real-time, includes information on metropolitan price tiers. This report offers valuable insights into the housing market and is crucial for understanding trends in the real estate sector.

data.title: Home prices in Los Angeles-Long Beach-Glendale area down 1.4% year over year in Sept as per First American Data & Analytics report.
data.description: First American Data & Analytics, a division of First American Financial Corporation (NYSE: FAF), based in Santa Ana, California, has recently unveiled its latest September 2025 Home Price Index (HPI) report. This report provides insights into property-centric information, risk management, and valuation solutions. The HPI report offers data on home price changes nationally, by state, and at the metropolitan level (Core-Based Statistical Area), with updates available less than four weeks behind real-time. The comprehensive report also includes information on metropolitan price tiers.

data.title: Sonic Fire Tech secured $3.5M to launch the world's first infrasound fire defense solutions, backed by Khosla Ventures and other investors.
data.description: In Cleveland, Sonic Fire Tech, a startup specializing in firetech and revolutionizing wildfire protection, recently secured $3.5 million in seed funding to expand its innovative infrasound fire suppression solutions. The funding round saw significant contributions from Khosla Ventures, Third Sphere, and AirAngels, as well as a consortium of angel investors sharing the company's mission. Sonic Fire Tech's acoustic suppression systems, aimed at averting ignition even before fires begin, provide non-destructive, waterless, and chemical-free safeguarding for residences.

data.title: Houston-The Woodlands-Sugar Land home prices decreased by 0.6% YoY in September 2025 per First American Data & Analytics report.
data.description: First American Data & Analytics, a division of First American Financial Corporation (NYSE: FAF), headquartered in Santa Ana, California, has revealed its latest September 2025 Home Price Index (HPI) report. The report offers insights into property-centric information, risk management, and valuation solutions. It closely monitors home price variations nationally, at state levels, and in metropolitan areas (Core-Based Statistical Areas), providing information on different metropolitan price tiers. The data is updated less than four weeks behind real-time, offering up-to-date analytics for the market.

data.title: Altai Resources Inc. completes a special cash distribution of $0.06 per common share, amounting to $3.4 million in total. Company now has $0.7 million cash left and zero debt.
data.description: Altai Resources Inc. announced the completion of a special cash distribution of $0.06 per common share, totaling approximately $3.4 million, funded from the company's cash and equivalents. With approximately $0.7 million remaining as of today, this amounts to roughly $0.013 per common share based on the total of 56,033,552 outstanding shares. Additionally, as of the same date, Altai Resources Inc. has no outstanding debt.

data.title: Azumo chosen as one of eight companies to advance in Amazon Devices Climate Tech Accelerator program. #sustainableinnovation
data.description: A technology company, Azumo, has secured a spot among the eight selected companies to progress in the Amazon Devices Climate Tech Accelerator program held in Chicago.

data.title: Carr’s Hill secures new capital facility with Crestline to boost Arrow Waste growth in the Southeastern U.S.
data.description: In New Orleans, Carr’s Hill Capital Partners Management, LP (CHP), a private equity firm specializing in industrial and infrastructure services in the Southeastern U.S., has finalized a new senior credit facility with Crestline Investors, Inc. for its portfolio company, Arrow Waste. This successful deal will grant Arrow substantial capital to boost its organic growth plan and support additional acquisitions. This development marks a significant milestone for Arrow Waste's expansion initiatives in the region.

data.title: Evergy plans to release 2025 third quarter earnings on Nov 6, 2025 before market open, with a conference call at 9:00 am Eastern.
data.description: Evergy, Inc. (NASDAQ: EVRG) from Kansas City, Missouri, has scheduled the release of its third-quarter earnings for 2025 on Thursday, November 6, 2025, before the market opens. The company will be conducting a conference call and audio webcast to discuss these results on the same day at 9:00 a.m. Eastern (8:00 a.m. Central). To access the webcast and presentation slides, individuals can visit the specified location provided by the company.
